metadata.purpose: Machine-readable temporal geography layer for the Zimmerman-Dible ancestry artifact.
metadata.append_policy: Append new event features, family-link features, and route sequence entries as research advances. Preserve existing ids unless correcting an error.
metadata.date_policy: Use ISO dates when known. Use year or year-month only when the source is less specific. Store date_sort for timeline ordering.

precision_values.unincorporated_community: Unincorporated community or named rural settlement.
precision_values.town_or_county_unresolved: A source may mean either a town/city or the surrounding county; coordinates use the best current point while awaiting original records.
precision_values.region: State, province, historic region, or broad locality.
precision_values.unresolved: Known place name is not yet geocoded safely.

confidence_values.documented: Directly supported by obituary, cemetery, indexed record, or specific record collection.
confidence_values.strong: Repeated public tree/index/cemetery evidence with consistent people, places, and dates.
confidence_values.lead: Specific compiled or indexed evidence worth mapping, but still awaiting original-record confirmation.
confidence_values.working: Useful compiled lead or indirect evidence; needs original proof.
confidence_values.possible: Hypothesis or possible-parent lead that must stay visually distinct from stronger evidence.
confidence_values.target: Record-search location rather than a proven life event.
confidence_values.open: Research target, not a proven life event.

metadata.geocoding_note: Coordinates are approximate modern locations from OpenStreetMap Nominatim lookups on 2026-07-06 unless coordinate_source says unresolved or manual_centroid. Historic boundaries are not modeled yet.

candidate_places.rationale: Sabanka is unresolved in Meyers/GOV/Nominatim; Neu Lebehnke / Nowa Lubianka, Kreis Deutsch Krone, Westpreussen has known Lebehnke parish/civil registry jurisdictions, local Zodrow cluster evidence, Lebehnke Catholic banns collateral entries, and a Johann Zodrow property-file catalog hit.

properties.label: Neu Lebehnke / Nowa Lubianka candidate for Sabanka, Prussia
properties.place_label: Neu Lebehnke / Nowa Lubianka, Kreis Deutsch Krone, Westpreussen
properties.notes: Use as a focused search target, not as a corrected birthplace, until John M. Zodrow's baptism, marriage, naturalization, or obituary confirms the locality. The research handle is no longer just 'Sabanka': search Neu Lebehnke / Nowa Lubianka for the village, Lebehnke / Stara Lubianka for Catholic/Lutheran parish and civil registration, and Kreis Deutsch Krone for county-level records. Collateral targets now include Lebehnke banns naming Zodrow families in Neu/Alt Lebehnke and a Polish archive catalog entry for a Johann Zodrow property file in Neu Lebehnke.

properties.label: Sabanka clue, usable Neu Lebehnke / Lebehnke / Deutsch Krone search stack

properties.evidence_note: Rust-family forum says Mary Frances Rust went by Frances and was born 6 Jun 1894, possibly in Smith County, Kansas.
FamilySearch's public Walter William Mundell page gives spouse Frances Rust and a 1910 marriage at Smith Center, Kansas.

properties.label: Mary Frances Rust Smith County parentage target
properties.research_question: Do Smith County birth, school, census, probate, marriage, cemetery, or newspaper records prove Mary Frances Rust as sister of William Bryan Rust and Clarence Aison Rust, and daughter of John F. Rust and Ethel B. Rupert?
properties.evidence_note: The forum clue names Frances's birth date, possible Smith County birthplace, and a brother William/Bill whose wife was Viola. Public search snippets for William Bryan Rust and Clarence Aison Rust point to Smith County, Kansas, and name parents John F. Rust and Ethel B. Rupert; William Bryan Rust's Find a Grave search result lists Mary Frances Rust Strawn, 1894-1984, as a sibling. This is a candidate cluster, not parent proof.

properties.label: John F. Rust and Ethel B. Rupert Reamsville parentage cluster
properties.place_label: Reamsville / Pleasant Hill Cemetery, Martin Township, Smith County, Kansas
properties.evidence_note: Reamsville Cemetery transcription lists F. John Rust, 1870-1909, and a nearby Smith County Rust cluster including John A. Rust and Nomdje N. Rust. Search snippets for William Bryan Rust and Clarence Aison Rust point to John F. Rust and Ethel B. Rupert as parents, while the William Bryan Rust Find a Grave search result lists Mary Frances Rust Strawn as a sibling.
properties.research_question: Do Smith County census, cemetery, marriage, guardianship, probate, and newspaper records prove this Reamsville John F. Rust/Ethel B. Rupert family as Mary Frances Rust's birth household?

properties.label: Frances Rust Adolph to Mary Frances Rust Strawn Beloit identity target
properties.evidence_note: Rust-family forum says Mary Frances Rust married an Adolph and, after he died, married Lyle Strawn and lived in Beloit, Kansas. William Bryan Rust's Find a Grave search result lists a sibling Mary Frances Rust Strawn, 1894-1984. Evelyn's obituary independently says she was raised by grandmother Frances Adolph.
properties.research_question: Do Beloit/Mitchell County marriage, obituary, cemetery, probate, city directory, or newspaper records prove Frances Adolph and Mary Frances Rust Strawn are the same person?

properties.evidence_note: Evelyn's obituary says she was raised by grandmother Frances Adolph and graduated from Winona High School.

properties.label: Frances Rust to Frances Adolph Winona identity target
properties.evidence_note: Rust-family forum says Mary Frances Rust later married a man surnamed Adolph; Evelyn's obituary says she was raised by grandmother Frances Adolph and graduated from Winona High School. These two clues fit, but same-person proof is still missing.
properties.research_question: Do Winona and Logan County school, census, guardianship, cemetery, probate, obituary, newspaper, or remarriage records prove that Frances Adolph was Mary Frances Rust Mundell and explain Evelyn's childhood household?

properties.label: Cantwell/Mundell Ohio record target
properties.notes: Barnabas Cantwell and Sarah Alice Cantwell proof target.

properties.label: Marjorie Opal Clemans same-name collision, Indiana
properties.place_label: Liberty Mills and South Bend, Indiana
properties.exclusion_note: FamilySearch and a compiled Eby genealogy describe Marjorie Opal Clemans as an Indiana woman born in Liberty Mills on 1 Mar 1914 and married to Clay V. Stamper in South Bend on 23 Oct 1935. This conflicts with the working Colorado/Kansas context for Evelyn's mother Marjorie Clemans Mundell and should not be merged without direct proof.
properties.research_question: Do Evelyn's birth, Homer/Marjorie marriage/divorce, census, or obituary records prove Marjorie's parents and exclude the Indiana Marjorie Opal Clemans profile?

properties.label: Dible/Heckman/Moore/Haden Pennsylvania record cluster
properties.place_label: Kiskiminetas Township, Armstrong County, and Westmoreland County, Pennsylvania
properties.notes: Probate, land, church, cemetery, and guardianship target for Dible, Heckman, Moore, Haden, Allshouse, and Otterman lines. Catherine Moore's exact dates conflict across compiled/index sources, so Kiskiminetas Township records are a priority.

properties.label: Catherine Moore identity/date conflict target
properties.conflict_note: Ancestry's John Moore Dible profile gives Catherine Moore 1839-1864; WikiTree's Harry Dible profile gives about 1834-6 Jun 1872. Original local records are needed before assigning exact dates or parents.

properties.label: Elizabeth J. Haden Cumberland County parentage target
properties.research_question: Do Cumberland County census, marriage, deed, probate, cemetery, or Bible records prove Elizabeth J. Haden as daughter of Andrew Jackson Haden and Sophia Reid/Reed, and Andrew as son of Richard D. Haden and Sarah Sally Lewis Tompkins?
properties.evidence_note: Find a Grave names Andrew Jackson Haden and Sophia Reid as Elizabeth's parents. KaysAncestry agrees, uses Sophia Reed, cites 1850/1860 Cumberland County census households, and its Andrew Jackson Haden page notes a Cumberland County mortgage to Sally L. Haden as Andrew's mother.
KaysAncestry's Richard D. Haden page gives a 7 Mar 1799 Albemarle County marriage to Sarah Sally Lewis Tompkins and notes bondsman/minister details.

properties.label: Richard D. Haden and Sarah Sally Lewis Tompkins Virginia-to-Kentucky record chain
properties.place_label: Albemarle, Fluvanna, and Bedford Counties, Virginia; Cumberland County, Kentucky
properties.evidence_note: KaysAncestry places Richard and Sally in Albemarle marriage records, notes possible Richmond/Fluvanna/Bedford tax context, and places their deaths in Cumberland County, Kentucky. The Andrew page names them as Andrew's parents and includes a Sally L. Haden deed clue.
properties.research_question: Do Albemarle marriage bonds, Virginia tax lists, Cumberland County deeds, and Cumberland probate records prove the Richard/Sally-to-Andrew Jackson Haden connection?

properties.label: Sophia Reed/Reid Cumberland County parentage target
properties.evidence_note: KaysAncestry notes Robert Reid, age 69, in Andrew and Sophia Haden's 1860 household and Edwin Reid, age 50, in Sophia's 1870 household, but presents them only as possible father/brother clues.
properties.research_question: Do Cumberland County marriage, probate, land, tax, cemetery, or census records identify Sophia Reed/Reid's parents and explain Robert Reid and Edwin Reid in the Haden households?

properties.label: Albrecht Heckman immigrant-origin lead
properties.notes: Beyond displayed Gen 6; sibling memorial for Ludwig Heckman points to Albrecht Heckman of Bodigheim/Baden.

properties.label: David Monroe Durham identity and parentage cleanup target

evidence_conflict.wku_identity: David Monroe Durham, born 28 Dec 1832 in North Carolina, died 24 Aug 1897 in Nebo, Kentucky, married Virginia Ann Ford 19 Aug 1857 in Menard County, Illinois.
evidence_conflict.ford_family_letter: Rev. G. W. Ford's 1911 letter says his sister Virginia Ann Ford was born 27 Dec 1842 and married David M. Durham of North Carolina in February 1858.
evidence_conflict.familysearch_child_page: Son David Milton Durham page calls father David Monroe Durham Jr. and mother Virginia Ann Ford.
evidence_conflict.findagrave_child_snippet: Mary Isabella Durham Hibbs snippet names father David M. Durham and mother Virginia Ann Ford, with variant father dates 1829-1887.

properties.research_question: Do original Menard County marriage, Hopkins/Muhlenberg death, cemetery, probate, and land records identify David Monroe Durham's exact dates and parents, and explain the Jr. label?

properties.evidence_note: Rev. G. W. Ford's 1911 letter gives the exact 11 Oct 1811 Caswell County birth; later queries repeat 1811 and treat his parents as unresolved.
Rev. G. W. Ford's 1911 letter says John Laborn Ford rode from North Carolina to Muhlenberg County in 1839, first stopping at Greenville before meeting Lucy Ann Staples at Friendship church.

properties.label: John Laborn Ford and Lucy Ann Staples marriage date conflict
properties.place_label: Friendship / Greenville area, Muhlenberg County, Kentucky

evidence_conflict.ford_family_letter: Rev. G. W. Ford's 1911 letter says John Laborn Ford and Lucy Ann Staples married in April 1840.
evidence_conflict.compiled_index_leads: WikiTree and Ancestry-style public snippets point to a 30 Mar 1842 Kentucky marriage.

properties.research_question: Do Muhlenberg County marriage books, bonds, minister returns, or Friendship Missionary Baptist Church records identify the correct marriage date and witnesses?

properties.evidence_note: Rev. G. W. Ford's 1911 letter says John Burton Staples and Nancy Ann Turner moved from Virginia to East Tennessee about 1830 before settling in Muhlenberg County in fall 1835.
Rev. G. W. Ford's 1911 letter says the Staples family settled about three miles southeast of Greenville in fall 1835.
Rev. G. W. Ford's 1911 letter says John Burton Staples died in June 1867 and that he and Nancy Ann Turner were members of Friendship Missionary Baptist Church.
Rev. G. W. Ford's 1911 letter gives Nancy Ann Turner as born in Virginia in November 1782.
Rev. G. W. Ford's 1911 letter says Nancy Ann Turner Staples died in February 1882, aged 99, and identifies Friendship Missionary Baptist Church as the family church.

properties.label: John Laborn Ford parentage conflict: John Laborn Ford Sr. or Levi Ford with Aritta Davis
properties.place_label: Scotland, Caswell County North Carolina, East Tennessee, and Muhlenberg County Kentucky

candidate_parent_sets.label: 1911 family-letter tradition
candidate_parent_sets.summary: Rev. G. W. Ford's 1911 letter says the elder John Laborn Ford was born in Scotland in 1767, Aritta Davis in Scotland in 1770, they married in 1793, came to Caswell County around 1797, and Aritta died three days after the younger John Laborn Ford was born on 11 Oct 1811.

candidate_parent_sets.label: Later Levi Ford hypothesis
candidate_parent_sets.summary: A 1983 Muhlenberg County Heritage query and the Caswell County Historical Association note raise Levi Ford as a possible father, but both frame the parentage as unproved and in need of empirical documentation.

properties.research_question: Do Caswell County marriage bonds, deeds, estate papers, guardianship records, tax lists, or court minutes prove whether John Laborn Ford, born 1811, was son of John Laborn Ford Sr. or Levi Ford, and identify Aritta Davis's origin?
properties.notes: This is a conflict/record target, not an attached proved ancestor layer.

properties.research_question: Is Hogle a transcription variant/error for Hoyle, a different legal surname, or a clue to Mary Margaret's parentage or prior household?
